metadata.source: CMS 2026 Medicare Physician Fee Schedule + CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals CY2023 (public data)
metadata.disclaimer: Rates are national Medicare averages. Actual reimbursement varies by locality, payer, and contract. Procedure descriptions are original summaries, not AMA CPT descriptions.
metadata.drg_data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023 release

codes.procedure_name: Endovascular cerebral thrombectomy
codes.description: Percutaneous revascularization of intracranial vessel occlusion using mechanical devices. Includes catheterization, diagnostic angiography, imaging guidance, and completion angiography.
codes.opps_note: Not paid under OPPS — inpatient only
codes.notes: Comprehensive code — bundles catheter placement, angiography, mechanical thrombectomy, and completion imaging. Most common code for acute ischemic stroke intervention.
codes.clinical_context: Acute ischemic stroke due to large vessel occlusion (LVO). Time-critical — typically within 24h of symptom onset per guideline criteria.

codes.procedure_name: Intra-arterial cerebral pharmacologic infusion — first territory
codes.description: Prolonged administration of pharmacologic agent into first intracranial vascular territory via catheter.
codes.opps_note: Not paid under OPPS — inpatient only
codes.notes: Used in conjunction with mechanical thrombectomy (61645) when thrombolytic agent is administered intra-arterially. May be billed in addition to 61645.
codes.clinical_context: Adjunctive intra-arterial thrombolysis during stroke intervention.

codes.procedure_name: Intra-arterial cerebral pharmacologic infusion — each additional territory
codes.description: Each additional intracranial vascular territory receiving prolonged pharmacologic infusion. Add-on code to 61650.
codes.opps_note: Not paid under OPPS — inpatient only
codes.notes: Add-on code — must be billed with 61650. Used when multiple vascular territories are treated.
codes.clinical_context: Multi-territory intra-arterial thrombolysis.

codes.procedure_name: Temporary balloon occlusion of intracranial artery
codes.description: Tracheobronchial stent placement — used in neurovascular context for balloon test occlusion of intracranial arteries.
codes.opps_note: Not paid under OPPS — inpatient only
codes.notes: Balloon test occlusion performed to assess collateral circulation before planned vessel sacrifice.
codes.clinical_context: Pre-treatment evaluation for giant or complex intracranial aneurysms.

codes.procedure_name: Endovascular intracranial embolization or occlusion
codes.description: Transcatheter permanent occlusion or embolization of intracranial vessels. Includes radiological supervision and interpretation (bundled in 2026).
codes.opps_note: Not paid under OPPS — inpatient only
codes.notes: 2026 CHANGE: Codes 75894 and 75898 (RS&I) are now bundled into 61624 — they can no longer be billed separately. Primary code for flow diversion procedures and parent vessel occlusion. For intracranial aneurysm coil embolization, use 61624 (NOT 61710 which is open surgery). Add 61626 for each additional vessel treated.
codes.clinical_context: Cerebral aneurysm treatment via flow diversion, AVM embolization, or parent vessel sacrifice. This is the PRIMARY code for endovascular coil embolization of intracranial aneurysms. Use for all transcatheter intracranial embolization procedures including aneurysm coiling, AVM embolization, and tumor embolization.

codes.procedure_name: Endovascular intracranial embolization — each additional vessel
codes.description: Each additional intracranial vessel treated with transcatheter embolization or occlusion. Add-on to 61624.
codes.opps_note: Not paid under OPPS — inpatient only
codes.notes: Add-on code to 61624 for each additional vessel treated in the same session.
codes.clinical_context: Multiple-vessel embolization in a single session.

codes.procedure_name: Trephination for subdural implantation of transducer
codes.description: Open surgical trephination for subdural implantation of transducer. This is an OPEN SURGICAL procedure, NOT endovascular. Do NOT use for endovascular coiling — use 61624 instead.
codes.opps_note: Not paid under OPPS — inpatient only
codes.notes: Primary code for endovascular coiling of intracranial aneurysms. Stent-assisted coiling uses this code plus 61624 for the stent deployment.
codes.clinical_context: Open craniotomy procedure for implantation of intracranial monitoring devices. NOT appropriate for endovascular coil embolization.

codes.procedure_name: Percutaneous arteriovenous fistula creation
codes.description: Percutaneous creation of arteriovenous fistula using thermal resistance energy.
codes.opps_note: Paid under OPPS at APC rate
codes.notes: Vascular access creation — not primary neurovascular but relevant for dialysis access in stroke patients.
codes.clinical_context: Hemodialysis access creation.

codes.procedure_name: Selective catheterization of internal carotid artery
codes.description: Selective catheter placement in the internal carotid artery, including all associated diagnostic imaging.
codes.opps_note: Paid under OPPS at APC rate
codes.notes: Bundled code — includes all catheter placement, contrast injection, and imaging. Separately reportable from 61645 when diagnostic only.
codes.clinical_context: Diagnostic cerebral angiography or access for neurointerventional procedures.

codes.procedure_name: Selective catheterization of subclavian/vertebral artery
codes.description: Selective catheter placement in the subclavian or vertebral artery, including associated diagnostic imaging.
codes.opps_note: Paid under OPPS at APC rate
codes.notes: Used for posterior circulation access. Includes imaging S&I. Separately billable from 36224 when different vessel.
codes.clinical_context: Posterior circulation stroke or vertebrobasilar diagnostic angiography.

codes.procedure_name: Selective catheterization of vertebral artery
codes.description: Selective catheter placement in the vertebral artery including all imaging.
codes.opps_note: Paid under OPPS at APC rate
codes.notes: For selective vertebral artery catheterization when performed as standalone diagnostic procedure.
codes.clinical_context: Vertebral artery diagnostic evaluation or access for basilar artery intervention.

codes.procedure_name: Selective catheterization of external carotid artery
codes.description: Selective catheter placement in the external carotid artery. Add-on code to 36224.
codes.opps_note: Add-on code — packaged with primary procedure
codes.notes: Add-on to 36224. Used when external carotid is selectively catheterized in addition to internal carotid.
codes.clinical_context: External carotid angiography during diagnostic workup.

codes.procedure_name: Selective catheterization of intracranial artery — each additional
codes.description: Selective catheter placement in each additional intracranial branch beyond first-order. Add-on code.
codes.opps_note: Add-on code — packaged with primary procedure
codes.notes: Add-on — reported per additional intracranial branch catheterized (e.g., MCA, ACA, PCA superselective).
codes.clinical_context: Superselective intracranial catheterization for targeted treatment or diagnostics.

codes.procedure_name: Primary percutaneous transluminal mechanical thrombectomy — non-coronary
codes.description: Primary mechanical thrombectomy of a non-coronary arterial or bypass graft, including fluoroscopic guidance.
codes.opps_note: Paid under OPPS at APC rate
codes.notes: Peripheral mechanical thrombectomy code. While 61645 is used for intracranial thrombectomy, 37184 applies to extracranial and peripheral vessels. Some payers may cross-reference.
codes.clinical_context: Extracranial or peripheral arterial thrombectomy.

codes.procedure_name: Mechanical thrombectomy — second and subsequent vessels
codes.description: Each additional vessel treated with mechanical thrombectomy in the same session. Add-on to 37184.
codes.opps_note: Add-on code
codes.notes: Add-on to 37184 for additional vessels treated with mechanical thrombectomy.
codes.clinical_context: Multi-vessel peripheral thrombectomy.

codes.procedure_name: Secondary percutaneous transluminal thrombectomy
codes.description: Secondary mechanical thrombectomy of non-coronary vessel, includes fluoroscopic guidance. Repeat procedure code.
codes.opps_note: Paid under OPPS at APC rate
codes.notes: Used for repeat/secondary thrombectomy attempts in same vessel on same day.
codes.clinical_context: Repeated thrombectomy attempt when initial pass is insufficient.

codes.procedure_name: Endovascular intracranial balloon angioplasty
codes.description: Balloon angioplasty of intracranial vasospasm or stenosis, percutaneous.
codes.opps_note: Not paid under OPPS — inpatient only
codes.notes: Used for treatment of cerebral vasospasm post-SAH or intracranial atherosclerotic stenosis.
codes.clinical_context: Cerebral vasospasm treatment after subarachnoid hemorrhage, or intracranial atherosclerotic disease.

codes.procedure_name: Endovascular intracranial stent placement
codes.description: Placement of intracranial stent, percutaneous, including angioplasty when performed.
codes.opps_note: Not paid under OPPS — inpatient only
codes.notes: Used for stent-assisted coiling or standalone intracranial stenting. Includes angioplasty when performed.
codes.clinical_context: Stent-assisted aneurysm coiling or intracranial atherosclerotic stenosis treatment.

codes.procedure_name: Angiography follow-through (RS&I) — historical
codes.description: Radiological supervision and interpretation for angiography through existing catheter.
codes.opps_note: BUNDLED into 61624 starting 2026 — no longer separately billable for intracranial embolization
codes.notes: 2026 CHANGE: Previously billed separately with 61624 for RS&I. Now bundled into 61624. Still separately billable for non-intracranial procedures.
codes.clinical_context: Follow-up angiographic imaging during interventional procedures.

codes.procedure_name: MRA head without contrast then with contrast
codes.description: Magnetic resonance angiography of the head without contrast, then with contrast and further sequences.
codes.opps_note: Paid under OPPS
codes.notes: Non-invasive diagnostic imaging. Often used for initial stroke workup or follow-up after thrombectomy/embolization.
codes.clinical_context: Pre-procedure or post-procedure cerebrovascular imaging.

codes.procedure_name: CTA head with contrast
codes.description: Computed tomographic angiography of the head with contrast, including image post-processing.
codes.opps_note: Paid under OPPS
codes.notes: First-line diagnostic imaging for acute stroke. Identifies LVO to determine thrombectomy candidacy.
codes.clinical_context: Emergent stroke evaluation — identifies large vessel occlusion.

codes.procedure_name: CTA neck with contrast
codes.description: Computed tomographic angiography of the neck with contrast, including image post-processing.
codes.opps_note: Paid under OPPS
codes.notes: Often performed with 70496 (CTA head) for complete cervicocerebral evaluation in acute stroke.
codes.clinical_context: Carotid and vertebral artery evaluation in stroke workup.

drg_codes.description: Craniotomy with Major Device Implant or Acute Complex CNS PDx with MCC or Chemo Implant or Epilepsy with Neurostimulator
drg_codes.neurovascular_context: Assigned for open craniotomy aneurysm clipping with MCC (e.g., SAH, hydrocephalus). Rarely applies to endovascular procedures.
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

drg_codes.description: Craniotomy with Major Device Implant or Acute Complex CNS PDx without MCC
drg_codes.neurovascular_context: Open craniotomy clipping without major comorbidities.
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

drg_codes.description: Craniotomy and Endovascular Intracranial Procedures with MCC
drg_codes.neurovascular_context: Primary DRG for endovascular coiling, flow diversion, and mechanical thrombectomy when patient has MCC (SAH, respiratory failure, sepsis).
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

drg_codes.description: Craniotomy and Endovascular Intracranial Procedures with CC
drg_codes.neurovascular_context: Endovascular intracranial procedures with complication/comorbidity (e.g., hypertension, diabetes).
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

drg_codes.description: Craniotomy and Endovascular Intracranial Procedures without CC/MCC
drg_codes.neurovascular_context: Elective endovascular procedures (unruptured aneurysm coiling/flow diversion) without significant comorbidities.
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

drg_codes.description: Ischemic Stroke, Precerebral Occlusion or Transient Ischemia with Thrombolytic Agent with MCC
drg_codes.neurovascular_context: IV tPA only (no thrombectomy). If thrombectomy performed, DRG 025-027 takes precedence.
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

drg_codes.description: Ischemic Stroke, Precerebral Occlusion or Transient Ischemia with Thrombolytic Agent without MCC
drg_codes.neurovascular_context: IV tPA only without major comorbidities.
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

drg_codes.description: Intracranial Hemorrhage or Cerebral Infarction with MCC
drg_codes.neurovascular_context: Medical management of SAH or stroke without intervention. If procedure performed, should code to procedural DRG.
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

drg_codes.description: Intracranial Hemorrhage or Cerebral Infarction with CC or tPA in 24 Hours
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

drg_codes.description: Intracranial Hemorrhage or Cerebral Infarction without CC/MCC
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

drg_codes.neurovascular_context: Carotid endarterectomy or carotid stenting with MCC.
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

drg_codes.neurovascular_context: Carotid endarterectomy or carotid stenting with CC.
drg_codes.data_source: CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023
CMS Medicare Inpatient Hospitals by Provider and Service, CY2023

endovascular_coiling.notes: DRG 025 for ruptured aneurysm (SAH = MCC). DRG 027 for elective unruptured aneurysm without comorbidities.

flow_diversion.notes: Pipeline/FRED/Surpass flow diversion. Same DRG family as coiling. MCC severity drives tier.

mechanical_thrombectomy.notes: DRG 025 for LVO stroke with MCC (common given stroke severity). Thrombectomy procedure takes precedence over medical stroke DRGs.

thrombectomy_with_tpa.notes: When both IV tPA and mechanical thrombectomy performed, procedural DRG (025-027) takes precedence over medical tPA DRG (061-062).

iv_tpa_only.notes: IV thrombolysis only, no endovascular intervention. DRG 061 with MCC, 062 without.

open_clipping.notes: Open craniotomy for aneurysm clipping. DRG 023 with MCC, 024 without.

carotid_stenting.notes: Carotid artery stenting or endarterectomy. Severity tiers by MCC/CC status.

diagnostic_angiography_only.notes: Diagnostic cerebral angiography without therapeutic intervention. Groups to medical DRG based on principal diagnosis.

metadata.source: CMS Hospital Cost Reports (HCRIS), MedPAR, AHA Annual Survey — 2024-2025 data
metadata.disclaimer: Costs are national averages and vary significantly by hospital, region, and case complexity. Device costs are list prices; hospitals negotiate discounts of 20-50%.

indirect_cost_components.description: Hospital indirect costs are overhead expenses not directly attributable to a single patient encounter but necessary for hospital operations. CMS uses a cost-to-charge ratio (CCR) methodology to estimate these.

overhead_and_administration.description: Hospital administration, billing, HR, legal, compliance, executive salaries

facility_and_plant.description: Building depreciation, maintenance, utilities, housekeeping, security

nursing_and_staffing.description: ICU nursing (neuro-ICU typical for neurovascular), floor nursing, techs, transport
nursing_and_staffing.notes: Neurovascular cases require neuro-ICU (higher nurse:patient ratio 1:1 or 1:2). ICU days are the #1 cost driver.

pharmacy.description: Medications including heparin, contrast dye, anesthesia drugs, IV tPA (alteplase/tenecteplase), antihypertensives, anticonvulsants

lab_and_diagnostics.description: Blood work (CBC, BMP, coags, troponin), CT/CTA, MRI/MRA, repeat imaging

anesthesia_services.description: Anesthesiologist, CRNA, monitoring, general anesthesia or conscious sedation for neurointerventional procedures
anesthesia_services.notes: General anesthesia typical for thrombectomy and complex coiling. Conscious sedation used for some elective cases.

capital_equipment_depreciation.description: Biplane angiography suite ($2-4M amortized), CT scanner, MRI, patient monitors, anesthesia machines

device_and_supply_costs.description: Direct device and disposable supply costs for neurovascular interventional procedures. List prices shown; hospitals typically negotiate 20-50% discounts depending on volume and GPO contracts.

aspiration_catheters.notes: Primary aspiration catheter for ADAPT technique. Often used alongside stent retrievers.

stent_retrievers.notes: Used alone (stent retriever first-line) or combined with aspiration. Multiple passes may require multiple devices.

intracranial_coils.notes: Wide range depending on aneurysm size. Large/giant aneurysms may use 20+ coils. HydroCoils and bioactive coils at premium.

flow_diverters.notes: Single device per case typical. May telescope 2 devices for long-segment coverage. Requires dual antiplatelet therapy.

intracranial_stents.notes: Used as scaffold for stent-assisted coiling of wide-neck aneurysms.

microcatheters.notes: Required for all endovascular neurovascular procedures. Multiple may be used per case.

guide_catheters_and_sheaths.notes: Tri-axial or bi-axial systems. Includes guide catheters, intermediate catheters, delivery catheters.

embolic_agents.notes: Used for AVM embolization and some aneurysm treatment. Multiple vials may be needed.

balloons.notes: Balloon-assisted coiling, angioplasty, or temporary occlusion.

mechanical_thrombectomy_adapt.typical_devices: Aspiration catheter + microcatheter + guidewire + guide/intermediate catheter + closure

mechanical_thrombectomy_stentriever.typical_devices: Stent retriever + aspiration catheter + microcatheter + guidewire + guide + closure

coil_embolization_small.typical_devices: 4-8 coils + microcatheter + guidewire + guide catheter + closure

coil_embolization_large.typical_devices: 10-20 coils + stent + microcatheter(s) + guidewires + guide + closure

flow_diversion.description: Flow diverter deployment for wide-neck/fusiform aneurysm
flow_diversion.typical_devices: Flow diverter + delivery catheter + microcatheter + guidewire + guide + closure

carotid_stenting.description: Carotid artery stenting with embolic protection
carotid_stenting.typical_devices: Carotid stent + embolic protection device + balloon + guide/sheath + closure

total_hospital_cost_vs_reimbursement.description: Comparison of total hospital costs (direct + indirect) vs DRG reimbursement. Negative margin means the hospital loses money on the case.

mechanical_thrombectomy.margin_notes: Thrombectomy is typically a net loss for hospitals. Justified by stroke center certification requirements, downstream admissions, and community benefit. Volume helps negotiate better device pricing.

coil_embolization_ruptured.margin_notes: Ruptured aneurysms have extended ICU stays and high complication rates. SAH patients often MCC tier. Transfer agreements and certification drive volume.

coil_embolization_elective.margin_notes: Elective cases are more predictable. Shorter LOS helps. Large aneurysms with many coils push costs higher.

flow_diversion_elective.margin_notes: Flow diverters are expensive devices. One-and-done vs. multi-stage coiling may reduce total episode cost. Sales angle: fewer OR returns.

carotid_stenting.margin_notes: Better margin than intracranial procedures due to lower device costs and shorter stays.

cost_reduction_strategies.description: How hospitals manage neurovascular case economics
strategies: Volume-based device pricing: 300+ cases/year = 30-50% device discounts
Standardized tray packs: pre-built catheter/wire kits reduce waste and open time
Efficient case flow: parallel processing of imaging, reducing anesthesia time
ICU avoidance protocols: step-down unit for low-risk elective cases
Dual antiplatelet compliance programs: reduce re-treatment rates for flow diversion
Consignment inventory: no capital tied up in device shelf stock
GPO contracting: leverage group purchasing for better pricing
Observation status: appropriate use of outpatient observation for elective CAS

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of unspecified precerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Use for AIS due to precerebral artery thrombosis when specific vessel not documented.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of right vertebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Posterior circulation stroke. May involve basilar territory.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of left vertebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Posterior circulation stroke. May involve basilar territory.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of basilar artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Basilar artery occlusion. High morbidity/mortality. EVT per ATTENTION/BAOCHE trials.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of right carotid artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: ICA thrombosis. Large vessel occlusion — primary EVT indication.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of left carotid artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: ICA thrombosis. Large vessel occlusion — primary EVT indication.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to embolism of unspecified precerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Embolic stroke from precerebral source.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to embolism of right vertebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Posterior circulation embolic stroke.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to embolism of left vertebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Posterior circulation embolic stroke.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to embolism of basilar artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Basilar embolism. High acuity — urgent EVT consideration.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to embolism of right carotid artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Carotid embolism. LVO — primary EVT indication.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to embolism of left carotid artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Carotid embolism. LVO — primary EVT indication.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of unspecified cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Intracranial artery thrombosis. Specify vessel and laterality when known.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of right middle cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: MCA occlusion — most common LVO target for mechanical thrombectomy.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of left middle cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: MCA occlusion — most common LVO target for mechanical thrombectomy.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of right anterior cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: ACA territory infarction. Less common EVT target.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to thrombosis of right posterior cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: PCA territory infarction. Posterior circulation.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to embolism of right middle cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: MCA embolism — primary thrombectomy indication.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to embolism of left middle cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: MCA embolism — primary thrombectomy indication.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction due to unspecified occlusion or stenosis of unspecified cerebral artery
icd10_codes.specificity_note: Use when mechanism (thrombosis vs embolism) and vessel are not specified
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Non-specific. Code to highest specificity available.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral infarction, unspecified
icd10_codes.specificity_note: Least specific — use only when etiology and vessel are truly unknown
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Unspecified AIS. Payers may query for more specific coding.

icd10_codes.description: Nontraumatic subarachnoid hemorrhage from unspecified carotid siphon and bifurcation
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: SAH from ICA aneurysm. Typically treated with coiling or clipping.

icd10_codes.description: Nontraumatic subarachnoid hemorrhage from unspecified middle cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: SAH from MCA aneurysm.

icd10_codes.description: Nontraumatic subarachnoid hemorrhage from anterior communicating artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: AComm aneurysm — most common location for ruptured aneurysms.

icd10_codes.description: Nontraumatic subarachnoid hemorrhage from unspecified posterior communicating artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: PComm aneurysm rupture. Second most common aneurysm location.

icd10_codes.description: Nontraumatic subarachnoid hemorrhage from basilar artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Basilar tip aneurysm rupture. Complex endovascular or surgical approach.

icd10_codes.description: Nontraumatic subarachnoid hemorrhage from unspecified intracranial artery
icd10_codes.specificity_note: Use when ruptured aneurysm location not yet identified
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: SAH with unspecified source. Angiography needed to localize.

icd10_codes.description: Nontraumatic subarachnoid hemorrhage, unspecified
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Unspecified SAH. Update when angiography results available.

icd10_codes.description: Nontraumatic intracerebral hemorrhage in hemisphere, subcortical
icd10_codes.specificity_note: Deep hemispheric (basal ganglia, thalamus, internal capsule)
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Deep ICH. Often hypertensive. Rarely requires endovascular intervention.

icd10_codes.description: Nontraumatic intracerebral hemorrhage in hemisphere, cortical
icd10_codes.specificity_note: Lobar hemorrhage — consider amyloid angiopathy or AVM
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Lobar ICH. May warrant angiography to evaluate for underlying AVM.

icd10_codes.description: Nontraumatic intracerebral hemorrhage in brain stem
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Brainstem hemorrhage. High morbidity. Usually managed conservatively.

icd10_codes.description: Nontraumatic intracerebral hemorrhage, intraventricular
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: IVH. May require EVD placement. Consider aneurysm or AVM workup.

icd10_codes.description: Occlusion and stenosis of right vertebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Vertebral stenosis/occlusion. May present as posterior circulation TIA or stroke.

icd10_codes.description: Occlusion and stenosis of left vertebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Vertebral stenosis/occlusion.

icd10_codes.description: Occlusion and stenosis of basilar artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Basilar stenosis. Risk of devastating posterior circulation stroke.

icd10_codes.description: Occlusion and stenosis of right carotid artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Carotid stenosis. CEA or CAS per guidelines.

icd10_codes.description: Occlusion and stenosis of left carotid artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Carotid stenosis. CEA or CAS per guidelines.

icd10_codes.description: Occlusion and stenosis of right middle cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: MCA occlusion/stenosis. If acute, primary EVT target.

icd10_codes.description: Occlusion and stenosis of left middle cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: MCA occlusion/stenosis. If acute, primary EVT target.

icd10_codes.description: Occlusion and stenosis of right anterior cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: ACA occlusion. Less common EVT target.

icd10_codes.description: Occlusion and stenosis of right posterior cerebral artery
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: PCA occlusion. Posterior circulation.

icd10_codes.description: Occlusion and stenosis of cerebellar arteries
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Cerebellar artery occlusion. Posterior circulation stroke risk.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral aneurysm, nonruptured
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Unruptured aneurysm. Treatment per size/location/risk per ISUIA/PHASES guidelines.

icd10_codes.description: Moyamoya disease
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Progressive steno-occlusive disease. May require bypass surgery.

icd10_codes.description: Cerebral arteritis, not elsewhere classified
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: CNS vasculitis. Angiography for diagnosis.

icd10_codes.description: Arteriovenous malformation of cerebral vessels
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Cerebral AVM. Treatment options: embolization, radiosurgery, microsurgery.

icd10_codes.description: Other malformations of cerebral vessels
icd10_codes.specificity_note: Includes dural AV fistula (dAVF), congenital cerebral aneurysm
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: dAVF or other vascular malformation. Endovascular embolization is primary treatment for dAVF.

icd10_codes.description: Vertebro-basilar artery syndrome
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Posterior circulation TIA. Evaluate vertebrobasilar stenosis.

icd10_codes.description: Carotid artery syndrome (hemispheric)
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Carotid TIA. Urgent carotid imaging. CEA or CAS if significant stenosis.

icd10_codes.description: Transient cerebral ischemic attack, unspecified
icd10_codes.specificity_note: Use when TIA territory not specified
icd10_codes.clinical_notes: Unspecified TIA. Requires vascular workup.

metadata.source: Published cost ranges from peer-reviewed literature and CMS data — not contract pricing.
metadata.disclaimer: Prices shown are published ranges from health economics literature, CMS OPPS/cost reports, and industry analyses. Actual hospital costs vary based on GPO contracts, volume commitments, and regional factors. Ranges represent approximate hospital acquisition costs after typical discounts.

classifications.label: Sheath
classifications.description: Vascular access sheath for femoral or radial arterial approach

products.name: Cook Pinnacle Destination Sheath
products.notes: Standard short sheath for femoral access

products.name: Merit Prelude Sheath
products.notes: Standard short sheath

products.name: Terumo Glidesheath Slender
products.notes: Thin-wall sheath — effectively one French size smaller OD. Popular for radial access

products.name: Cook Shuttle Select Sheath
products.notes: Long sheath for stable proximal access. Used as shuttle for guide catheter delivery

products.name: Teleflex Arrow Sheath
products.notes: Standard femoral access sheath

products.name: Terumo Glidesheath Slender Radial
products.notes: Hydrophilic thin-wall design for distal radial (snuffbox) access

classifications.label: Guide Catheter
classifications.description: Large-bore catheter providing stable access and flow arrest/reversal capability

products.name: Stryker FlowGate2 Balloon Guide Catheter
products.notes: Balloon guide catheter for flow arrest during thrombectomy. Dual lumen

products.name: Medtronic Walrus Balloon Guide Catheter
products.notes: Balloon guide catheter for flow control

products.name: Penumbra Benchmark Catheter
products.notes: Non-balloon guide catheter. Good trackability for tortuous anatomy

products.name: Stryker AXS Infinity Plus
products.notes: Large-bore distal access catheter. Can be used as guide or intermediate

products.name: Medtronic Neuron MAX
products.notes: Long guide catheter with good distal reach

products.name: MicroVention Sophia Plus
products.notes: Flexible guide catheter

classifications.label: Select / Diagnostic Catheter
classifications.description: Shaped catheter for selective vessel catheterization during diagnostic angiography

products.name: Cook Simmons Catheter
products.notes: Simmons-1, -2, -3 curves for aortic arch catheterization. Standard workhorse

products.name: Terumo Glidecath
products.notes: Hydrophilic diagnostic catheter. Multiple shapes (Simmons, JB, Vertebral)

products.name: Merit Impress Catheter
products.notes: Diagnostic catheter, multiple curve options

products.name: Cordis Tempo Catheter
products.notes: Standard diagnostic catheter

products.name: Cook Berenstein Catheter
products.notes: Berenstein curve for selective catheterization

classifications.label: Intermediate / Distal Access Catheter
classifications.description: Flexible large-bore catheter for distal intracranial access, bridging guide and aspiration catheters

products.name: Stryker AXS Catalyst 7
products.notes: Distal access catheter. Often used as intermediate in triaxial setups

products.name: Penumbra ACE 68
products.notes: Intermediate/distal access catheter

products.name: MicroVention SOFIA
products.notes: Flexible distal access catheter

products.name: Cerenovus NeuroSlider
products.notes: Distal access catheter for triaxial systems

products.name: Penumbra 3D Separator
products.notes: Intermediate catheter. Frequently used in triaxial setups with ACE/JET aspiration systems

products.name: Penumbra ACE 64
products.notes: Smaller-bore variant of ACE series. Used as intermediate or aspiration in smaller vessels

classifications.label: Aspiration Catheter
classifications.description: Large-bore intracranial catheter for direct aspiration thrombectomy (ADAPT technique)

products.name: Penumbra JET 7 Reperfusion Catheter
products.notes: Largest-bore aspiration catheter. Primary tool for ADAPT technique. Used with Penumbra ENGINE

products.name: Penumbra RED 72 Reperfusion Catheter
products.notes: Latest generation aspiration catheter from Penumbra

products.name: Stryker AXS Vecta 71
products.notes: Large-bore aspiration catheter for direct aspiration or combined technique

products.name: MicroVention SOFIA Flow Plus
products.notes: Flow-directed aspiration catheter. Good distal trackability

products.name: Medtronic React 71
products.notes: Aspiration catheter with optimized suction dynamics

products.name: Penumbra JET 71
products.notes: Size variant of JET series. Slightly smaller bore than JET 7 (0.072")

classifications.label: Stent Retriever
classifications.description: Self-expanding stent device deployed across thrombus, integrates with clot, then retrieved with microcatheter

products.name: Stryker Trevo XP ProVue Retriever
products.notes: Most widely used stent retriever. Full visibility under fluoroscopy

products.name: Medtronic Solitaire X Revascularization Device
products.notes: Established stent retriever. Proven in ESCAPE, EXTEND-IA trials

products.name: Penumbra Lightning Flash
products.notes: Newer generation stent retriever

products.name: MicroVention ERIC Retriever
products.notes: Non-laser-cut stent retriever design

products.name: Cerenovus Embotrap III
products.notes: Dual-layer design for clot capture

classifications.label: Microcatheter
classifications.description: Small-bore catheter for distal intracranial navigation and device delivery

products.name: Medtronic Marksman Microcatheter
products.notes: Standard microcatheter for stent retriever delivery. Compatible with Solitaire

products.name: Stryker Excelsior XT-27 Microcatheter
products.notes: Workhorse microcatheter for thrombectomy device delivery

products.name: Boston Scientific Renegade High-Flow Microcatheter
products.notes: High-flow microcatheter for embolization procedures. Good pushability

products.name: Medtronic Echelon 10 Microcatheter
products.notes: Small-bore microcatheter for coil delivery and distal navigation

products.name: Stryker Excelsior SL-10 Microcatheter
products.notes: Pre-shaped tip options for aneurysm coiling

products.name: MicroVention Headway 17 Microcatheter
products.notes: Versatile microcatheter for coiling and stent delivery

products.name: Penumbra PX Slim Microcatheter
products.notes: Low-profile microcatheter for distal access

classifications.label: Microwire
classifications.description: Ultra-thin guidewire for navigating intracranial vasculature through microcatheters

products.name: Stryker Synchro-2 Microwire
products.notes: Standard workhorse microwire. Good torque and trackability

products.name: Medtronic Transend Microwire
products.notes: Floppy-tip microwire for atraumatic distal navigation

products.name: Stryker Synchro SELECT Microwire
products.notes: Enhanced tip shaping for complex anatomy

products.name: Balt Hybrid Microwire
products.notes: Nitinol core microwire

products.name: MicroVention Traxcess Microwire
products.notes: Hydrophilic microwire for distal access

classifications.label: Guidewire
classifications.description: Standard guidewire for catheter exchanges and navigation in cervical/extracranial vessels

products.name: Terumo Glidewire
products.notes: Hydrophilic standard guidewire. Most commonly used exchange wire

products.name: Terumo Glidewire Advantage
products.notes: Enhanced hydrophilic coating for reduced friction

products.name: Cook Amplatz Super Stiff
products.notes: Extra-stiff guidewire for sheath/catheter exchanges

products.name: Boston Scientific V-18 Control Wire
products.notes: Intermediate wire for catheter navigation

products.name: Abbott Hi-Torque Pilot
products.notes: Coronary-style wire used in intracranial work

classifications.label: Intracranial Coils
classifications.description: Detachable platinum coils for aneurysm occlusion (packing) and vessel sacrifice

products.name: Stryker Target Detachable Coils
products.notes: Per coil. Typical case uses 6-15 coils ($8K-$25K total). Electrolytic detachment

products.name: MicroVention COSMOS Coils
products.notes: Per coil. PGLA-coated options for enhanced packing

products.name: Medtronic Axium Detachable Coils
products.notes: Per coil. Instant mechanical detachment

products.name: Penumbra POD Coils
products.notes: Per coil. Volume-based pricing available

products.name: Cerenovus Cerecyte Coils
products.notes: Per coil. Polymer-enhanced for bioactive response

classifications.label: Flow Diverter
classifications.description: Endoluminal device that diverts flow away from aneurysm sac, promoting thrombosis and vessel remodeling

products.name: Medtronic Pipeline Flex Embolization Device
products.notes: Most widely used flow diverter. Gold standard per PUFS trial

products.name: MicroVention FRED (Flow Re-Direction Endoluminal Device)
products.notes: Dual-layer design with inner flow diverter and outer stent

products.name: Stryker Surpass Streamline Flow Diverter
products.notes: Woven mesh design. SCENT trial data

products.name: Balt SILK Vista Baby Flow Diverter
products.notes: Low-profile flow diverter for distal/small vessels

products.name: Stryker Surpass Evolve Flow Diverter
products.notes: Next-generation Surpass platform. Improved deliverability and conformability over Streamline

classifications.label: Intracranial Stent
classifications.description: Self-expanding stent for vessel remodeling, stent-assisted coiling, or intracranial stenosis

products.name: Stryker Neuroform Atlas Stent
products.notes: Open-cell design. Most common stent-assisted coiling platform

products.name: MicroVention LVIS Stent
products.notes: Braided design. Good wall apposition. Can be resheathed

products.name: Medtronic Enterprise 2 Stent
products.notes: Closed-cell design for stent-assisted coiling

products.name: Stryker Wingspan Stent
products.notes: Only FDA-approved stent for intracranial atherosclerotic stenosis (ICAS)

classifications.label: Embolic Agents
classifications.description: Liquid embolics, particles, and other agents for vessel occlusion and tumor devascularization

products.name: Medtronic Onyx Liquid Embolic System
products.notes: Per vial. EVOH copolymer. Standard for AVM/dAVF embolization. Multiple vials common

products.name: MicroVention PHIL Liquid Embolic
products.notes: Per vial. Non-adhesive copolymer. Iodine-based visibility

products.name: Boston Scientific Contour PVA Particles
products.notes: Per syringe. PVA particles for tumor/meningeal embolization. Used in MMA embolization for SDH

products.name: Merit Embosphere Microspheres
products.notes: Per syringe. Calibrated microspheres for precise embolization

products.name: Medtronic n-BCA (Trufill)
products.notes: Per vial. Cyanoacrylate glue for high-flow AVM feeders

classifications.label: Balloon Catheter
classifications.description: Intracranial or cervical balloon for remodeling, angioplasty, or temporary flow control

products.name: MicroVention Scepter XC Balloon
products.notes: Dual-lumen remodeling balloon for balloon-assisted coiling

products.name: Medtronic HyperForm Balloon
products.notes: Compliant balloon for remodeling and vessel test occlusion

products.name: Medtronic HyperGlide Balloon
products.notes: Over-the-wire compliant balloon for angioplasty

products.name: Stryker NeuroSpeed PTA Balloon
products.notes: Semi-compliant balloon for intracranial angioplasty

products.name: Medtronic Gateway PTA Balloon
products.notes: Only FDA-approved balloon for intracranial stenosis (used with Wingspan)

classifications.label: Closure Device
classifications.description: Arterial puncture site closure device for hemostasis post-procedure

products.name: Terumo TR Band
products.notes: Radial artery compression band. Most common radial closure

products.name: Terumo AngioSeal VIP
products.notes: Collagen plug + anchor closure for femoral access

products.name: Abbott Perclose ProGlide
products.notes: Suture-mediated closure. Can pre-close for large-bore access

products.name: Teleflex MANTA VCD
products.notes: Large-bore closure device for 10F-18F sheaths

products.name: Cardinal Health Mynx Grip
products.notes: Sealant-based closure device
